top_signals.title: Russia launches massive strike on Ukraine as Poland scrambles jets, Finland restricts airspace
top_signals.summary: The Russian defence ministry said that it launched a "massive strike using long-range precision air, land, and sea-based weapons and attack drones."
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: OpenAI proposes 5% stake to Trump administration to ease Washington pressure: report
top_signals.summary: Trump said in June that the U.S. taking an ownership stake in AI giants would be "a beautiful thing" and make American public "partners in this revolution."
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: Policy paper: FSCS Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements 2025/26
top_signals.summary: In its Annual Report and Accounts and Class Statements published today (Thursday 2 July, 2026), the Financial Services Compensation Scheme (FSCS) outlines the vital work it has carried out during the past financial year.

top_signals.title: Why Trucking Companies Should Hire an Insurance Agent—Not Just Buy Insurance
top_signals.summary:

top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Members of rebel Catholic group in schism and excommunicated, Vatican says
top_signals.summary: The Vatican said on Thursday that priests and lay Catholics who are part of a breakaway right-wing Catholic group that ordained bishops without Pope Leo's approval are in schism with the wider church and now excommunicated.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: U.S. International Trade in Goods and Services, April 2026
top_signals.summary: The U.S. monthly international trade deficit decreased in April 2026 according to the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis and the U.S. Census Bureau. The deficit decreased from $56.6 billion in March (revised) to $55.9 billion in April, as exports increased more than imports. The goods deficit decreased $2.4 billion in April to $83.7 billion. The services surplus decreased $1.7 billion in April to $27.8 billion. Full Text

top_signals.title: Worse Than an Axis
top_signals.summary: The informal alignment of U.S. adversaries is dangerous.

top_signals.title: Europe Goes Its Own Way
top_signals.summary: Drifting from America, the continent Is rearming and reordering itself.

top_signals.title: Opera rolls out Paste Protect feature to fight ClickFix attacks
top_signals.summary: Opera has introduced Paste Protect, a security feature designed to block ClickFix-style attacks that trick users into executing malicious commands through social engineering. [...]

top_signals.title: House passes kids’ online safety bill, but Senate approval unlikely
top_signals.summary: The Kids Internet and Digital Safety (KIDS) Act passed with bipartisan support by a 267-117 margin, winning the two-thirds majority needed to greenlight the legislation under a process that speeds up a bill’s path to a vote but requires more than a simple majority.
